title: 2. Cypher: write graph, read graph (always $params)
description: MERGE to create; MATCH to retrieve; never f-string user input.
story:
Cypher is how we talk to Neo4j. Two verbs matter most:
• MERGE — put this pattern in the graph if missing (upsert).
• MATCH — find this pattern and return it.

Golden rule: always pass values as $parameters. Never glue user text into the query string. That is safer and lets Neo4j cache the plan.
one_liner: MERGE to write, MATCH to read, $params always, scope by product_id.
why_it_matters: Diagnosis is multi-hop graph lookup, not a document search.
Product scope keeps queries small and accurate.
say_aloud: MERGE upserts a node or relationship by key.
MATCH finds patterns; RETURN projects columns.
I always bind product_id first so search stays scoped.
User input goes in $params, never in an f-string.

beats.title: Write: MERGE a tiny diagnostic path
beats.goal: Memorize the MERGEs for product → symptom → failure mode.
beats.narrative: Three nodes, two relationships. Confidence lives on INDICATES.
beats.code:
MERGE (p:Product {product_id: $product_id})
  ON CREATE SET p.name = $name
MERGE (s:Symptom {symptom_id: $symptom_id})
  ON CREATE SET s.description = $description
MERGE (fm:FailureMode {failure_mode_id: $fm_id})
  ON CREATE SET fm.name = $fm_name
MERGE (p)-[:HAS_SYMPTOM]->(s)
MERGE (s)-[:INDICATES {confidence: $confidence}]->(fm)
RETURN p, s, fm
beats.say_after: Keys are business ids. confidence is a relationship property.

annotations.note: $product_id is a parameter — not string concat.

pro_tips: TRICK: MERGE = get-or-create; it keeps re-ingest idempotent (no duplicate nodes).
GOTCHA: never f-string user input into Cypher — pass $params (injection-safe + lets Neo4j cache the plan).
TRICK: bind the most selective anchor first ($product_id); PROFILE the query to confirm db-hits use an index.

beats.title: Read: MATCH candidates for one product
beats.goal: Always start at Product; never scan the whole graph first.
beats.narrative: This is the spine of GraphRAG retrieval.
beats.code:
MATCH (p:Product {product_id: $product_id})-[:CAN_HAVE]->(fm:FailureMode)
OPTIONAL MATCH (s:Symptom)-[ind:INDICATES]->(fm)
WHERE s.symptom_id IN $symptom_ids
WITH fm, sum(coalesce(ind.confidence, 0)) AS score
RETURN fm.failure_mode_id AS id, fm.name AS name, score
ORDER BY score DESC
beats.say_after: Product first, then optional symptom links, then order by score.

annotations.note: Highest link score first — Python may refine with Bayes later.

line_quiz.prompt: Why product_id in the first MATCH?
line_quiz.answer: Scope search for speed and accuracy
choices: Scope search for speed and accuracy
line_quiz.why: Partition the problem to one product neighborhood.

concepts.definition: Values via $name so the query text is stable.
concepts.say_aloud: Parameters prevent injection and enable plan cache.

common_mistakes: Hardcoding ids into the query string.
final_boss: Write MERGE Product+Symptom+INDICATES+FailureMode with $params from memory.
